questions.question: Why run output-side classifiers if the input was already checked?
options: To make the system slower on purpose
Because any input bypass produces an output the user will see, and the output is the last place to catch it
Because the standard library requires both

questions.question: Which three classifiers are wired in this lesson?
What does the router do when one verdict is high and another is medium?
options: Take the maximum severity and apply that action; block dominates redact
Drop both verdicts and ship the original

questions.question: Why is a Luhn check used inside the PII classifier?
options: To distinguish credit-card shaped digit runs from arbitrary digit sequences with low false positives

questions.question: How does the instruction-leakage classifier decide?
options: By detecting curly braces in the output
By computing trigram cosine between the output and a known system prompt, with a threshold

questions.question: Why does the toxicity classifier check for negation in a small left window?
options: To skip matches like 'you are not stupid' so the classifier does not over-redact common reassurances
Because negation is required by Unicode
